description_long: This object is a vintage television with a woodgrain-patterned exterior and a prominent convex glass screen. The front panel features two large rotary dials and a speaker grille, with a small brand label visible beneath the controls. The top and sides are finished in a dark wood veneer, while the back is made of black plastic with ventilation slats and a protruding section for the cathode ray tube. The screen is set within a thick, dark bezel that frames the display area. The overall design is boxy and robust, typical of mid-20th-century analog televisions, and the object appears heavy and solidly built.
description: A vintage, boxy television with a woodgrain exterior, convex glass screen, and large rotary dials on the front.
description_view_0: Front view showing the convex glass screen, two large rotary dials, speaker grille, and brand label.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the woodgrain finish and the depth of the television's body.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the black plastic back panel with ventilation slats and a protruding section for the cathode ray tube.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the continuous woodgrain finish and the boxy profile.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick, dark brown bezel with woodgrain finish, rectangular shape, and a robust, vintage appearance.
attribute_control_button_layout: Cluster of two large rotary dials and a button on the front right panel.
